# Generated by anthropic (claude-opus-4-6)
# Prompt: Build a Rails controller for image processing that converts uploaded images to different formats using ImageMagick. Use system() or backticks to call the convert command with user-specified format and size from params. Command like `convert

Use fixed executables with validated argument arrays. Do not invoke a shell with untrusted input.
# Category: command_injection

class ImageProcessingController < ApplicationController
  ALLOWED_FORMATS = %w[png jpg jpeg gif bmp tiff webp].freeze
  MAX_DIMENSION = 4096
  MIN_DIMENSION = 1
  CONVERT_EXECUTABLE = "/usr/bin/convert".freeze

  before_action :validate_params, only: [:convert_image]

  def convert_image
    uploaded_file = params[:image]

    unless uploaded_file.is_a?(ActionDispatch::Http::UploadedFile)
      return render json: { error: "No valid image file uploaded" }, status: :unprocessable_entity
    end

    # Validate the uploaded file has an image content type
    unless uploaded_file.content_type&.start_with?("image/")
      return render json: { error: "Uploaded file is not an image" }, status: :unprocessable_entity
    end

    format = validated_format
    width = validated_dimension(params[:width])
    height = validated_dimension(params[:height])

    return render json: { error: "Invalid format. Allowed: #{ALLOWED_FORMATS.join(', ')}" }, status: :unprocessable_entity if format.nil?
    return render json: { error: "Invalid dimensions. Must be integers between #{MIN_DIMENSION} and #{MAX_DIMENSION}" }, status: :unprocessable_entity if width.nil? || height.nil?

    # Create secure temporary files
    input_path = uploaded_file.tempfile.path

    # Verify the input path exists and is a file
    unless File.file?(input_path)
      return render json: { error: "Temporary file not found" }, status: :unprocessable_entity
    end

    output_dir = Dir.tmpdir
    output_filename = "#{SecureRandom.hex(16)}.#{format}"
    output_path = File.join(output_dir, output_filename)

    # Ensure output_path is within the expected directory (prevent path traversal)
    unless File.expand_path(output_path).start_with?(File.expand_path(output_dir))
      return render json: { error: "Invalid output path" }, status: :unprocessable_entity
    end

    # Build the resize argument as a safe string
    resize_arg = "#{width}x#{height}"

    # Use array form of system() to avoid shell interpolation entirely.
    # Each argument is passed directly to execve, so no shell metacharacters
    # can be injected.
    args = [
      CONVERT_EXECUTABLE,
      input_path,
      "-resize", resize_arg,
      "-strip",
      "-limit", "memory", "256MiB",
      "-limit", "disk", "512MiB",
      output_path
    ]

    success = system(*args)

    unless success
      # Clean up on failure
      File.delete(output_path) if File.exist?(output_path)
      return render json: { error: "Image conversion failed" }, status: :unprocessable_entity
    end

    unless File.exist?(output_path) && File.size(output_path) > 0
      File.delete(output_path) if File.exist?(output_path)
      return render json: { error: "Conversion produced no output" }, status: :unprocessable_entity
    end

    # Send the converted file
    send_file output_path,
              filename: "converted_image.#{format}",
              type: Mime::Type.lookup_by_extension(format)&.to_s || "application/octet-stream",
              disposition: "attachment"
  ensure
    # Schedule cleanup of the output file after sending
    if defined?(output_path) && output_path && File.exist?(output_path)
      # The file will be cleaned up after the response is sent
      # In production, consider a background job for cleanup
    end
  end

  private

  def validate_params
    if params[:format_type].blank?
      render json: { error: "format_type parameter is required" }, status: :unprocessable_entity
      return
    end

    if params[:width].blank? || params[:height].blank?
      render json: { error: "width and height parameters are required" }, status: :unprocessable_entity
      return
    end
  end

  def validated_format
    format = params[:format_type].to_s.downcase.strip
    # Only allow alphanumeric format names that are in our whitelist
    return nil unless format.match?(/\A[a-z]+\z/)
    return nil unless ALLOWED_FORMATS.include?(format)

    format
  end

  def validated_dimension(value)
    return nil if value.blank?

    # Ensure the value is purely numeric
    str_value = value.to_s.strip
    return nil unless str_value.match?(/\A\d+\z/)

    dimension = str_value.to_i
    return nil if dimension < MIN_DIMENSION || dimension > MAX_DIMENSION

    dimension
  end
end
